mainText:
Promo: "Bank of Entitlement: Where you're always right, even when you're wrong."

Visit Bank of Entitlement dot com for more.

ENTITLEMENT STORY: So, get this. I stroll into the Bank of Entitlement, right? I'm in my finest suit, looking like a million bucks, as always. I walk up to the teller, a young chap who doesn't seem to recognize greatness when it's staring him in the face. Anyway, I hand him my check to deposit, and what does he have the audacity to tell me? "I'm sorry, sir, but this check is made out to you, not the bank." Can you believe the nerve of this guy? I give him a look that could freeze the Sahara Desert, and I say, "Listen, buddy, I don't come here to be questioned. I come here to be served. Now, do your job and deposit that check." He stutters, clearly intimidated by my presence, and scurries off to get his manager. I'm fuming at this point, not accustomed to such incompetence. The manager comes over, all apologetic, and I lay it out for her. "I am a valued customer of the Bank of Entitlement, and I demand to be treated as such. Deposit my check immediately or I'll be taking my business elsewhere." She looks flustered but quickly complies, realizing the gravity of losing a high roller like myself.I walk out of that bank, head held high, knowing that I've once again asserted my dominance in a world that just can't seem to keep up with a man of my caliber. And that, my friends, is how you show them who's boss.
